response:
Emily collected 24 blueberries from the bushes in the field. She carefully picked each one ensuring they were ripe and ready for use. Her collection was the starting point for the group's baking adventure. David was eager to contribute more than she did and he managed to collect 15 more than Emily. His efforts were impressive and showed his dedication to the task at hand.

***

David's total was 39 blueberries which was also three times the amount that Sophia collected. This meant Sophia had 13 blueberries which was a small but valuable contribution. Together their combined total was 24 plus 39 plus 13. This added up to 76 blueberries which they used for making muffins. The group worked efficiently to prepare the blueberries for the baking process.

***

Each batch of muffins required 9 blueberries and they were able to make several batches. They divided the total number of blueberries by 9 to determine how many batches they could make. The result was 8 full batches with 4 blueberries remaining unused. Each batch was sold for 5 dollars and they made a total amount from the sales. The money earned was calculated by multiplying the number of batches by the price per batch.

***

The total amount they made was 40 dollars from the muffins they sold. Their teamwork and effort paid off in the form of money and a sense of accomplishment. They shared the earnings and felt proud of their work. The experience taught them the value of collaboration and hard work. Youth is a time for learning and growing through such activities.
